summary: Bug fix verified: Frequency capping values (Max Impressions, Period, Type, Daily Cap, Lifetime Cap) are now correctly saved when updating a campaign via 'Update campaign' button. Backend API tests (9/9 passed) confirm all frequency cap fields are persisted to database. Frontend UI tests confirm values are correctly loaded and displayed after save.

issues:
Minor: HTML nesting warning -
inside

tag in Badge component - does not affect functionality

critical_code_review_comments: FrequencyCapConfig model in models.py correctly accepts both legacy fields (max_impressions_per_user, time_window_hours) and new fields (max_impressions, period, type, daily_cap, lifetime_cap)
useWizardForm.js buildCampaignPayload() correctly converts frequency cap values to integers using parseInt()
ScheduleStep.jsx uses type='text' with inputMode='numeric' and pattern='[0-9]*' for numeric inputs - this allows proper typing without immediate parseInt conversion
Daily Cap and Lifetime Cap fields are conditionally shown only when Cap Type is 'Per User' - this is expected behavior
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frequency capping bug fix verified. Test campaign ID: e74cfdc4-ae8d-40e3-9752-fd585020b09b (Demo Ak Freq cap). Current values in DB after testing: max_impressions=30, period=week, type=campaign, daily_cap=50, lifetime_cap=200. All frequency cap fields are correctly saved and loaded.

ui_verification: Period dropdown shows 'Per Week' after save (loaded from DB value 'week')
Cap Type dropdown shows 'Per Campaign' after save (loaded from DB value 'campaign')
Max Impressions input shows '30' after save
'Campaign updated successfully' toast appears after clicking Update campaign

rca_of_the_issue: Root cause was that the backend FrequencyCapConfig model had extra='ignore' and only accepted old field names (max_impressions_per_user, time_window_hours, etc.), but frontend was sending different fields (max_impressions, period, type). Fix: Updated FrequencyCapConfig model to accept both old and new field names. Also ensured frontend converts values to integers before sending using parseInt().
